rationale: The citing paper uses Pocheptsova, Labroo, and Dhar (2010) as part of its core disfluency theory, specifically to support that disfluency can produce positive inferences (e.g., specialness) rather than only negative effects. It also directly borrows an attribution manipulation in Study 3 (explicitly noted as adapted from that paper) to test whether attributing difficulty to its source attenuates the effect. In the discussion, the authors explicitly position their contribution relative to this prior finding by showing a different downstream consequence of disfluency.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on the cited paper's metacognitive-disfluency account and adapts its source-attribution approach to test mechanism. It extends that line of work from special-occasion evaluation effects to arrangement-induced exploratory search and increased choice of unfamiliar products.
